personality_one_liner: A sharp, minimal, and highly professional iOS-native financial command center anchored by crisp typography, white space, and deep navy blue accents.

spacing.rule: Use iOS-native spacing rhythms, comfortable vertical margins, and generous padding. Do not cram elements together. Forms should have clear grouping.

do's: Apply to sticky headers over scrolling content
Apply to bottom tab bar when content scrolls behind
Apply to FABs and modal scrims
dont's: Never apply to form fields, body text regions, or cards in dense lists

screens.name: Auth
screens.purpose: Email/password login and registration for the consulting team.

states.error: Error toast or readable message below input fields

screens.layout: Hero image at the top with a subtle gradient scrim (dark/light based on theme) transitioning into the form. Single column inputs with labels above. Large sticky primary CTA at the bottom inside KeyboardAvoidingView.

screens.name: Dashboard
screens.purpose: Financial command center displaying summary cards, chart, and recent activity.

states.loading: Skeleton loaders for cards and charts
states.empty: Illustration of an empty vault/wallet + 'No financial data yet. Start tracking income.'
states.error: Error message with a Retry button filling the screen

screens.layout: Scrollview. Top section: 6 summary cards arranged in a 2-column grid using flexWrap, space-between, and md gap. Middle: Income vs Expense chart. Bottom: List of 5 recent transactions. Use glassmorphism on the header.

screens.name: Income
screens.purpose: Track and manage consulting income with entry forms.

states.empty: Clean line icon + 'No income logged. Tap + to add.'

screens.layout: Native table view list. Top sticky filter row (chip row) for Search/Currency/Date. Items display Client Name, Date, Amount (with + sign and green color), and a Pending/Paid badge. FAB for adding new income.

screens.name: Expenses
screens.purpose: Track operating expenses grouped by category.

states.empty: Clean line icon + 'No expenses logged. Tap + to add.'

screens.layout: Similar to Income. Include small category icons inside brandTertiary rounded squares for each list row. Explicit - sign and dark text for amounts. FAB for adding new expense.

screens.name: Startup Costs

states.empty: Clean line icon + 'No startup costs logged. Tap + to add.'

screens.layout: List view with explicit categories (Legal, Equipment, etc.). Total startup cost pinned at the top in a large hero card. Floating action button to add.

screens.name: Reports
screens.purpose: Analytical view with monthly/yearly summaries and export options.

states.empty: Icon + 'Not enough data to generate reports.'

screens.layout: Segmented control at the top to toggle Monthly/Yearly/Category/Cash Flow. Below: relevant chart, followed by a breakdown list. Export CTA buttons (CSV/PDF) fixed at the bottom.

screens.name: Settings
screens.purpose: App configurations including dark mode, profile, and logout.
screens.layout: Grouped list style (iOS native settings). Rows for Dark Mode toggle, Multi-currency default, Profile Info, and a destructive Logout button at the bottom.

auth_hero.alt: Modern minimal office architecture for auth background

instructions_to_main_agent: Follow 1 iOS-Native Clean personality. Ensure generous padding, white space, and large tap targets (minimum 44x44pt).
Use the Navy Blue (#003366) brand color deliberately for primary accents and buttons, maintaining high contrast against white backgrounds. Do not overuse it on large surfaces in light mode.

Implement a 6-tab bottom navigation exactly as the user requested (Dashboard, Income, Expenses, Startup Costs, Reports, Settings). Since 6 tabs can be tight on mobile, ensure the font size adjusts to fit or use a highly compact, clean layout for the tab bar.
For the Dashboard, render the 6 summary cards using a 2-column flex layout (width roughly 48% with space-between).

In transaction lists (Income, Expenses), implement iOS multi-currency patterns: prominently display the currency code (EUR, TRY, GBP) or symbol right next to the amount. Use a positive sign (+) and green for income, and a negative sign (-) with dark grey/red for expenses.
Ensure the hero image on the Auth screen implements a mandatory gradient scrim (transparent to surface color) layered between the image and the form, ensuring the form remains perfectly readable.
All forms (Add Income, Add Expense, etc.) must have labels *above* the input fields, single-column layout, and a sticky Submit button anchored to the bottom inside a KeyboardAvoidingView.
Always wrap interactive elements in Pressable with a hitSlop if smaller than 44x44pt.
Apply Glassmorphism to sticky headers, tab bars, and bottom sheets where applicable to elevate the iOS-native feel.
For empty states, ensure they fill the screen (flex: 1) with a centered icon/illustration, a brief descriptive text, and a primary CTA.
